name: Alamo Square
vibe: The Painted Ladies, postcard panorama, park with dog walkers and tourists, between Western Addition and Hayes Valley

name: Bayview
vibe: Southeast SF, historically Black, Hunters Point naval shipyard redevelopment, community gardens, food deserts

name: Bernal Heights
vibe: Tight-knit hilltop community, dog park at the summit with panoramic views, artsy, family-oriented, good burritos

name: Castro
vibe: LGBTQ+ cultural center, Victorians, rainbow crosswalks, Harvey Milk legacy, arts, boutiques, Twin Peaks backdrop

name: Chinatown
vibe: Dragon gates, herbalists, dim sum, crowded streets, one of the oldest in the US, Grant Avenue tourism vs Stockton St locals

name: Civic Center
vibe: Government buildings, UN Plaza, Davies Symphony Hall, City Hall dome, homeless services, government workers

name: Cow Hollow
vibe: Upscale, Union Street shops and restaurants, between Marina and Pacific Heights, affluent but walkable

name: Dogpatch
vibe: Industrial waterfront being gentrified — craft breweries, design studios, historic buildings, T-Third streetcar

name: Embarcadero
vibe: Ferry Building, Ferry Plaza Farmers Market, Bay Bridge views, the waterfront promenade, Financial District edge

name: Excelsior
vibe: Working-class Latino neighborhood, Mission Street extension, affordable, McLaren Park, diverse immigrants

name: Fillmore
vibe: Fillmore Street strip, jazz history, music venues, sushi row, transitional between Japantown and Pacific Heights

name: Financial District
vibe: Suits, skyscrapers, lunch hustle, BART stations, transamerica pyramid, quiet on weekends

name: Fisherman's Wharf
vibe: Tourist epicenter — sea lions at Pier 39, crab stands, Alcatraz ferries, Ghirardelli Square, foghorns

name: Glen Park
vibe: Quiet village feel, canyon park, BART stop, more residential and calm than its neighbors

name: Haight-Ashbury
vibe: Summer of Love epicenter, head shops, Victorians, hippie legacy meets current vintage stores, close to GG Park

name: Hayes Valley
vibe: Boutiques, wine bars, Patricia's Green, opera-crowd cafes, gentrified after freeway removal, design-conscious

name: Inner Richmond
vibe: Clement Street — Asian restaurants, European bakeries, bookshops, fog belt, working class families

name: Inner Sunset
vibe: Irving Street cafes, foggy mornings, close to GG Park and UCSF, diverse families, N-Judah streetcar

name: Japantown
vibe: Japan Center mall, Peace Pagoda, ramen shops, sake bars, cultural programming, smaller than it once was

name: Lower Haight
vibe: Grittier sibling of Haight, bars and small venues, working class, tattooed locals, less touristy

name: Marina
vibe: Young professionals, brunches, Chestnut Street boutiques, Palace of Fine Arts, bay views, earthquake-prone fill

name: Nob Hill
vibe: Old money, grand hotels (Fairmont, Mark Hopkins), cable cars, Grace Cathedral, city views, very steep

name: Noe Valley
vibe: Quiet, affluent, stroller-dense, brunch culture, 24th Street commercial strip, valley warmth sheltered from fog

name: North Beach
vibe: Beat generation legacy, Italian restaurants, Vesuvio bar, City Lights bookstore, Coit Tower, tourists and old-timers

name: Outer Richmond
vibe: Quiet fog, Balboa Street, Lincoln Park, Baker Beach access, more residential and local than Inner Richmond

name: Outer Sunset
vibe: Foggy beach town within a city, surfers at Ocean Beach, Dutch windmills, Vietnamese restaurants, quiet

name: Pacific Heights
vibe: Mansions, consulates, city views, very expensive, Lafayette Park, 'Billionaires' Row' on Broadway

name: Portola
vibe: Between Excelsior and Bernal Heights, diverse, Mission Street commercial strip, relatively unknown to outsiders

name: Potrero Hill
vibe: Sunny hill with views, industrial past giving way to tech, good microclimate, dog walkers, converted warehouses

name: Russian Hill
vibe: Lombard Street, steep streets, city views, quiet residential, cable car routes, literary history

name: SoMa
vibe: South of Market — tech offices, SFMOMA, Moscone Center, leather bars, homeless encampments, nightclubs, live-work lofts

name: Sunset District
vibe: Broad umbrella for Outer/Inner Sunset — fog capital of SF, largely residential, N-Judah along Judah St

name: Tenderloin
vibe: Dense, difficult, resilient — immigrant restaurants, social services, SROs, Little Saigon, city's hardest edge

name: The Mission
vibe: Vibrant Latino heart of SF — murals, taquerias, Mission Dolores, gentrification tension, bike lanes, nightlife on Valencia

name: The Tenderloin
vibe: See Tenderloin

name: Union Square
vibe: Retail heart — Westfield mall, hotels, theaters, cable car turnaround, Powell BART, very tourist-dense

name: Visitacion Valley
vibe: Southern tip, working class, diverse Asian and Latino residents, McLaren Park, quiet residential

name: Western Addition
vibe: Historically Black neighborhood, Fillmore jazz legacy, painted ladies, mosaic of cultures after urban renewal
